text: The software shall maintain all components under a version control system.
The software shall include facilities for nearly automatic inclusion into the Gemini software environment.
The software shall restrict access to the system according to the current operational level.
The software shall provide monitoring access as a read-only case that does not affect the performance of ongoing observations.
The software shall support remote operations through a distributed environment.
The software shall support team observing with multiple observers at different sites.
The software shall restrict specific operations to specific remote sites.
The software shall ensure that no single point of control for the telescope exists for observing astronomers.
The software shall provide an automatic sequencer for observing operations.
The software shall support the sorting and resequencing of the observation queue.
The software shall provide a telescope simulator for testing science programs.
The software shall support a command syntax that is consistent across workstation and IOC software.
The software shall respond to a common set of commands for status testing and version inquiries.
The software shall adopt a uniform ACK/NAK protocol across all systems.
The software shall support command timeouts at approximately 500 msec.
The software shall ensure that handshaking of commands between IOCs occurs within 100-200 msec.
The LAN shall support a transfer rate of 20-40 Mbits/second.
The software shall store astronomical data using a standard compressed format.
The software shall retain 7 days of data produced by the largest instrument.
The software shall transmit rough video images every 0.5 sec.
The software shall implement an Access Mode Allocation system to protect against accidental interference.
The software shall accept or reject every command within 2 sec.
The software shall update local status displays within 4 sec.
The software shall log and fix all software bugs after detection.
The software shall provide built-in test (BIT) facilities to verify system performance.
The software shall notify users of specific faults including origin and problem description.
The software shall allow control transfer between user stations via a software reconfiguration procedure.
The software shall be written as machine-independent portable code for non-hardware-specific tasks.
The software shall verify the consistency of all software component versions at boot time.
The software shall support a total system downtime of no more than 2%.
The software shall perform range and validity checking before executing any input command.
The software shall recover or reconfigure within 5 minutes of an error condition.
The software shall bring the system to a safe state upon notification of danger.
The software shall provide a network gateway acting as a firewall for the astronomical database.
The software shall support database access times in the range of 2-3 msec.
The software shall use the Ward/Mellor approach for real-time systems development.
The development operating system shall be POSIX-compliant UNIX System V Release 4.
The software shall use Tcl/Tk as the principle language for developing Graphical User Interfaces.
The IOCs shall use the VxWorks real-time operating system.
The software shall use the DARPA TCP/IP communication protocol over LANs and the WAN.
